name: Planet Labs PBC

profile.description: Planet Labs operates the largest fleet of Earth observation satellites, imaging the entire landmass daily. 945 employees. Revenue growing 42.1% YoY with government and commercial geospatial intelligence customers.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Revenue grew 42.1% YoY driven by government contracts and commercial geospatial analytics expansion. Operates 200+ Earth observation satellites. AI Deployment (2/2): AI-powered planetary analytics platform processing daily global imagery for change detection and insights. Market Share (1/2): Largest commercial earth observation fleet but competes with Maxar and Airbus. Product Pipeline (1/2): Next-gen satellite fleet and analytics platform expansion. R&D Intensity (1/1): Significant satellite and analytics R&D.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (4/4): 'Use space to help life on Earth' — validated purpose driving environmental monitoring, disaster response, and agricultural analytics. Community Impact (1/2): Environmental and humanitarian applications. Employee Retention (0/2): 945 employees, no data. Insider Buying (0/2): No data.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Revenue growing 42.1% but deeply unprofitable with -111.17% margin and -83.99% ROE [1]. D/E of 110% reflects space infrastructure investment. No meaningful P/E available. Strong revenue growth offset by massive losses.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Physical satellite infrastructure in orbit cannot be replaced by AI — Earth observation requires actual hardware in space. AI enhances the analytics layer. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI-powered geospatial analytics platform generating revenue.

infinite_game.summary: Planet Labs has a genuine Just Cause in using space to help life on Earth, with consistent execution across satellite deployment. The environmental mission is deeply authentic.

investment_thesis: Planet Labs' 42.1% revenue growth reflects accelerating demand for daily global Earth observation imagery from government and commercial customers [1]. The 200+ satellite fleet creates an irreplaceable data moat.
thesis_continued: Deeply unprofitable with -111.17% margin and -83.99% ROE [1]. Space infrastructure is capital-intensive with ongoing satellite replacement needs. Competition from Maxar, Airbus, and new entrants.
key_catalysts: Government defense and intelligence contracts expansion
Commercial agriculture and insurance analytics adoption
Next-generation satellite fleet improving resolution and frequency
key_risks: Sustained deep losses with no clear path to profitability
Satellite replacement capex creating ongoing capital needs
sources: Yahoo Finance — PL Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
